family_audit.reason: Round 18 was a strong negative branch: switching the PTX grouped-row window from 4 to 8 regressed runtime from 24.17296028 ms to 24.53401566 ms and widened long-scoreboard from 7.13% to 7.33%. The immediate next move is therefore to restore the PTX winner surface before attempting another alternate family.
The grouped_rows=8 branch is now directly measured negative on the current code lineage and should not stay top-ranked.
This remains the strongest geometry-side fallback once the PTX restore is complete.
This stays in the live queue as another distinct family after the grouped_rows=8 miss.
The control-path family remains live in the frontier, but it now trails the direct restore and the broader alternate branches after one flat and one negative outcome.

directions.name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
directions.hypothesis: Round 18 provided clean negative evidence on the grouped_rows=8 alternate PTX surface. Runtime jumped from 24.17296028 ms to 24.53401566 ms, long-scoreboard widened from 7.13% to 7.33%, and the branch fell far outside the current 24.16-24.18 band. The source change for this round was intentionally narrow, so the loop should not stack another new family on top of the bad state. The next move should be to restore the PTX winner surface again, re-anchor the search on the known-good grouped_rows=4 implementation, and only then spend further rounds on alternate geometry or sibling-surface families.
directions.expected_bottleneck: The grouped_rows=8 locality regime itself is the measured problem here; the immediate need is to remove that drift and recover the proven PTX winner surface.
directions.risk: Low to moderate. This is a narrow restore from a clearly negative branch rather than a fresh speculative change.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus the 24.534016 ms current run, the 24.178592 ms accepted base, and the 24.164272 ms best-known run
correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
ranking_notes: This ranks first because the grouped_rows=8 branch is now a measured negative and the implementation delta is narrow enough to undo cleanly.
Restoring immediately avoids compounding new experiments on top of a bad state.
It also preserves the option to test 256x128 or the non-PTX sibling from a known-good baseline in the next rounds.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the source already matches the PTX winner surface or if the restore fails to recover a materially cleaner runtime signature.

directions.name: Promote The Existing 256x128 Pivot Hot-Band Kernel
directions.hypothesis: Once the grouped_rows=8 miss is backed out, the next strongest live alternate family is still the 256x128 pivot hot-band kernel. It represents the cleanest geometry-side fallback after the grouped_rows=8 miss and may reduce control amortization pressure by using a broader hot-band reuse tile than the 128x128 PTX branch.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Geometry-level latency hiding and control amortization on the 256x128 hot-band path rather than PTX grouped-row locality.
directions.risk: Medium to high. This is a broader dispatch swap onto a non-default geometry, so it carries more surface-change risk than dir_01.
ranking_notes: This stays second because it remains the strongest geometry-side family after the grouped_rows=8 branch was measured negative.
It ranks behind dir_01 because the immediate priority is still to restore off the bad grouped_rows=8 state.
It should remain the next alternate once the implementation surface is re-anchored.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the 256x128 promotion cannot get materially closer to the PTX winner band or if it opens a broad hot-band regression without counter improvements.

directions.name: Port grouped-row traversal into the non-PTX 128x128 sibling
directions.hypothesis: The queue should still carry one sibling-surface branch that is neither the PTX grouped_rows family nor the 256x128 pivot geometry. Porting grouped-row traversal into the non-PTX 128x128 sibling preserves the broad 128x128 footprint while changing the execution surface away from the PTX microkernel. It remains third because the grouped_rows=8 miss makes the direct PTX restore and the 256x128 geometry branch more urgent, but it is still worth preserving in the active queue.
directions.expected_bottleneck: CTA traversal and locality on the non-PTX 128x128 sibling surface rather than PTX microkernel control behavior.
directions.risk: Medium. This keeps the 128x128 footprint but moves away from the PTX winner surface, so upside depends on locality gains outweighing the sibling-surface cost.
ranking_notes: This keeps another distinct family alive after the grouped_rows=8 miss.
It ranks below dir_02 because 256x128 has stronger recent frontier support as the next geometry-side branch.
It still deserves the third slot to preserve queue breadth after the search-policy reset.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the non-PTX sibling remains clearly behind the PTX branch without showing a locality or scheduler advantage worth continuing.

notes: Round 19 treats grouped_rows=8 as clear negative evidence. The next move is a direct restore back to the PTX winner surface, while 256x128 pivot and the non-PTX 128x128 sibling remain live as the next two alternate families.
